title: Identifying and Closing Endpoint Visibility Gaps
content:
# Identifying and Closing Endpoint Visibility Gaps

## Overview

Endpoint telemetry gaps represent blind spots in security monitoring where malicious activity can occur undetected. Even organizations with mature security programs often have incomplete endpoint visibility due to agent deployment gaps, misconfigured logging, unsupported platforms, or insufficient data collection. Identifying and closing these gaps is essential for effective threat detection and incident response.

This guide provides a systematic approach to assessing endpoint telemetry coverage, identifying gaps, and implementing remediation to achieve comprehensive endpoint visibility.

## Endpoint Telemetry Sources

### Core Telemetry Categories

| Category | Data Collected | Primary Source | Detection Value |
|----------|---------------|----------------|----------------|
| Process execution | Process creation, command line, parent process, hash | EDR, Sysmon, auditd | Malware execution, LOLBin abuse, lateral movement tools |
| Network connections | Source/dest IP, port, protocol, process mapping | EDR, firewall, netflow | C2 communication, data exfiltration, lateral movement |
| File operations | File creation, modification, deletion, rename | EDR, Sysmon, auditd | Malware drop, data staging, log tampering |
| Registry changes | Key creation, modification, deletion | EDR, Sysmon | Persistence, configuration tampering, defense evasion |
| Authentication events | Logon success/failure, type, source | OS logs, AD logs | Brute force, credential stuffing, pass-the-hash |
| DNS queries | Query name, type, response, process | EDR, DNS server logs, Sysmon | C2 domains, DNS tunneling, DGA detection |
| Loaded modules | DLLs, drivers, kernel modules loaded | EDR, Sysmon | Process injection, rootkits, DLL hijacking |
| Script execution | PowerShell, Python, Bash content and output | Script logging, EDR | Fileless malware, encoded commands, download cradles |
| WMI/COM activity | WMI subscriptions, COM object creation | Sysmon, ETW | Persistence, lateral movement, fileless execution |
| Scheduled tasks/cron | Task creation, modification, execution | OS logs, Sysmon | Persistence, scheduled execution |
| USB/removable media | Device connection, file transfers | EDR, USB audit logs | Data exfiltration, unauthorized media, USB attacks |
| Certificate operations | Certificate install, modification, validation | OS logs, EDR | Man-in-the-middle, code signing abuse |

### Telemetry Source Comparison

| Telemetry | Windows Event Log | Sysmon | EDR | Value Add |
|-----------|-------------------|--------|-----|----------|
| Process creation | 4688 (limited) | Event 1 (rich) | Full context | Sysmon/EDR adds hashes, parent details, user context |
| Network connections | 5156 (firewall) | Event 3 | Full context | Sysmon/EDR maps connection to process |
| File creation | Limited | Event 11 | Full context | Sysmon/EDR adds hash, process that created file |
| Registry modification | 4657 (if audited) | Event 13 | Full context | Sysmon/EDR adds value content, process context |
| DNS queries | DNS debug log | Event 22 | Full context | Sysmon/EDR maps DNS to process |
| Process injection | None native | Event 8, 10 | Full context | Only Sysmon/EDR detect injection |
| Image/DLL loading | None native | Event 7 | Full context | Only Sysmon/EDR track module loads |
| WMI events | None (useful) | Event 19, 20, 21 | Partial | Sysmon uniquely tracks WMI persistence |

## Gap Assessment Methodology

### Step 1: Asset Inventory Reconciliation

Compare your endpoint security tool inventory against your asset management database:

```bash
# Export EDR agent inventory
# (Tool-specific: CrowdStrike, SentinelOne, Defender, etc.)
# Export as CSV: hostname, OS, agent_version, last_seen

# Export asset inventory from CMDB/AD
# Active Directory computer objects:
powershell -Command "Get-ADComputer -Filter * -Properties OperatingSystem,LastLogonDate | Select-Object Name,OperatingSystem,LastLogonDate | Export-Csv ad_computers.csv"

# Compare inventories to find gaps
python3 -c "
import csv
edr_hosts = set()
cmdb_hosts = set()
with open('edr_inventory.csv') as f:
    for row in csv.DictReader(f):
        edr_hosts.add(row['hostname'].upper())
with open('ad_computers.csv') as f:
    for row in csv.DictReader(f):
        cmdb_hosts.add(row['Name'].upper())
missing = cmdb_hosts - edr_hosts
print(f'Systems without EDR agent: {len(missing)}')
for host in sorted(missing):
    print(f'  {host}')
"
```

**Coverage gaps to identify**:

| Gap Category | Detection Method | Common Causes |
|-------------|-----------------|---------------|
| Missing EDR agent | CMDB vs. EDR inventory comparison | New systems not enrolled, failed deployment, shadow IT |
| Outdated EDR agent | Agent version check | Update failures, offline systems, blocked updates |
| Unhealthy EDR agent | Agent health status check | Tampered agent, resource constraints, driver conflicts |
| Missing log forwarding | SIEM vs. asset inventory | Misconfigured log shipping, network issues, capacity limits |
| Unsupported OS/platform | OS inventory analysis | Legacy systems, IoT, embedded systems, containers |
| Missing Sysmon | Sysmon presence check | Not deployed, removed, incompatible |

### Step 2: Telemetry Coverage Mapping

Map your telemetry coverage against the MITRE ATT&CK matrix to identify detection blind spots:

| ATT&CK Tactic | Required Telemetry | Your Coverage | Gap |
|---------------|-------------------|---------------|-----|
| Initial Access | Email logs, proxy logs, endpoint process creation | Assess | |
| Execution | Process creation with command line, script logging | Assess | |
| Persistence | Registry monitoring, scheduled task monitoring, file monitoring | Assess | |
| Privilege Escalation | Process token changes, service creation, exploit detection | Assess | |
| Defense Evasion | Process injection detection, module loading, file modification | Assess | |
| Credential Access | LSASS access monitoring, authentication logs, Kerberos logs | Assess | |
| Discovery | Process creation (enumeration commands), LDAP queries | Assess | |
| Lateral Movement | Authentication logs (type 3/10), remote service creation, file shares | Assess | |
| Collection | File access auditing, clipboard monitoring, screen capture detection | Assess | |
| Exfiltration | Network connections with volume, DNS queries, outbound traffic analysis | Assess | |
| Command and Control | Network connections, DNS queries, process-to-network mapping | Assess | |

### Step 3: Log Source Health Monitoring

```bash
# SIEM query: Check for log source dropoff
# Look for systems that stopped sending logs in the last 24 hours

# Example: Check EDR telemetry freshness
siem_query:
  index=edr_telemetry
  | stats max(timestamp) as last_seen by hostname
  | where last_seen < now() - 24h
  | sort by last_seen
  | rename hostname as "Silent Endpoint", last_seen as "Last Telemetry"

# Example: Check Windows Event Log forwarding
siem_query:
  index=windows_security
  | stats count by host, source
  | where count < expected_daily_minimum
  | sort by count asc
```

**Critical monitoring checks**:

| Check | Frequency | Alert Threshold |
|-------|-----------|----------------|
| EDR agent reporting | Every 15 minutes | No check-in for 1 hour |
| Windows Security log forwarding | Hourly | No events for 2 hours |
| Sysmon log forwarding | Hourly | No events for 2 hours |
| Linux auditd log forwarding | Hourly | No events for 2 hours |
| DNS log forwarding | Hourly | No events for 1 hour |
| Firewall log forwarding | Every 15 minutes | No events for 30 minutes |

### Step 4: Configuration Audit

Verify security tools are configured for maximum telemetry:

**Windows Audit Policy**:
```powershell
# Check current audit policy
auditpol /get /category:*

# Recommended minimum audit policy for security monitoring:
# Account Logon: Success and Failure
# Account Management: Success and Failure  
# Detailed Tracking (Process Creation): Success
# Logon/Logoff: Success and Failure
# Object Access: Success and Failure (for file/registry auditing)
# Policy Change: Success and Failure
# Privilege Use: Success and Failure
# System: Success and Failure

# Verify command line logging is enabled
reg query "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\Audit" /v ProcessCreationIncludeCmdLine_Enabled
# Should return 1

# Verify PowerShell script block logging
reg query "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\ScriptBlockLogging" /v EnableScriptBlockLogging
# Should return 1

# Verify PowerShell module logging
reg query "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\ModuleLogging" /v EnableModuleLogging
# Should return 1
```

**Sysmon Configuration Audit**:
```powershell
# Check if Sysmon is installed and running
Get-Service Sysmon64 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object Status, StartType

# Check Sysmon configuration
sysmon64 -c

# Verify critical events are being generated
# Event 1 (Process Create), 3 (Network), 7 (Image Load),
# 8 (CreateRemoteThread), 10 (ProcessAccess), 11 (FileCreate),
# 13 (RegistryEvent), 22 (DNSEvent)
Get-WinEvent -LogName "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" -MaxEvents 1 | Select-Object TimeCreated
```

**Linux Audit Configuration**:
```bash
# Check auditd status
sudo systemctl status auditd

# Review audit rules
sudo auditctl -l

# Minimum recommended audit rules:
# Process execution
# -a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S execve -k process_execution
# File integrity
# -w /etc/passwd -p wa -k identity
# -w /etc/shadow -p wa -k identity
# Network connections
# -a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S connect -k network_connect
# Privilege escalation
# -w /usr/bin/sudo -p x -k privilege_escalation

# Check if audit logs are being forwarded
ls -la /var/log/audit/audit.log
tail -5 /var/log/audit/audit.log
```

## Common Telemetry Gaps and Remediation

### Gap 1: No Process Command Line Logging

**Impact**: Cannot see what commands are being executed, severely limiting detection of malicious activity.

**Remediation**:
- Windows: Enable "Include command line in process creation events" via GPO
- Deploy Sysmon for enhanced process creation logging (Event ID 1)
- Linux: Enable auditd execve logging

### Gap 2: No DNS Query Logging Per Process

**Impact**: Cannot determine which process made a DNS query, limiting C2 detection.

**Remediation**:
- Deploy Sysmon with Event ID 22 (DNSEvent) enabled
- EDR solutions typically provide process-level DNS telemetry
- Enable DNS debug logging on DNS servers as a fallback

### Gap 3: No Process Injection Detection

**Impact**: Cannot detect in-memory attacks, reflective DLL loading, or process hollowing.

**Remediation**:
- Deploy Sysmon with Event IDs 8 (CreateRemoteThread) and 10 (ProcessAccess) enabled
- EDR solutions with behavioral detection can identify injection techniques
- Configure Sysmon ProcessAccess rules to monitor access to lsass.exe

### Gap 4: Missing Endpoint Coverage

**Impact**: Entire systems operating without security monitoring.

**Remediation**:
- Implement automated EDR deployment in system provisioning
- Deploy NAC to detect and quarantine unmanaged devices
- Run weekly CMDB-to-EDR reconciliation
- Set SLA: 24 hours from system deployment to EDR enrollment

### Gap 5: No Script Content Logging

**Impact**: Cannot see the actual content of scripts being executed, only that a script engine ran.

**Remediation**:
- Enable PowerShell Script Block Logging (Event ID 4104)
- Enable PowerShell Module Logging (Event ID 4103)
- Configure bash/zsh HISTTIMEFORMAT and forward history
- EDR solutions may provide native script content capture

### Gap 6: Container and Cloud Workload Blind Spots

**Impact**: Containerized workloads and cloud instances not monitored.

**Remediation**:
- Deploy container-aware EDR agents (eBPF-based for Linux)
- Implement cloud workload protection platforms (CWPP)
- Forward cloud audit logs (CloudTrail, Activity Log, Audit Logs) to SIEM
- Deploy Falco or similar runtime security for Kubernetes

### Gap 7: Encrypted Traffic Blind Spot

**Impact**: Cannot inspect TLS-encrypted traffic for C2 or exfiltration.

**Remediation**:
- Deploy TLS inspection at network egress (proxy/NGFW)
- Use endpoint-level EDR that captures pre-encryption network data
- Monitor for TLS certificate anomalies (self-signed, expired, unusual CAs)
- Analyze JA3/JA3S fingerprints for known-bad TLS implementations

## Telemetry Coverage Scorecard

Rate your organization's coverage in each area:

| Telemetry Area | Coverage Level | Score |
|---------------|---------------|-------|
| | None: No collection | 0 |
| | Partial: Some systems or limited data | 1 |
| | Majority: Most systems, good data quality | 2 |
| | Complete: All systems, comprehensive data, SIEM integrated | 3 |

| Area | Windows | Linux | macOS | Score |
|------|---------|-------|-------|-------|
| Process creation with command line | | | | /9 |
| Network connections per process | | | | /9 |
| File creation/modification/deletion | | | | /9 |
| Registry/config changes | | | | /9 |
| Authentication events | | | | /9 |
| DNS queries per process | | | | /9 |
| Module/DLL loading | | | | /9 |
| Script content logging | | | | /9 |
| Process injection detection | | | | /9 |
| Scheduled task/cron monitoring | | | | /9 |

**Score interpretation**:
- 80-90: Excellent visibility
- 60-79: Good visibility with notable gaps
- 40-59: Moderate visibility, significant blind spots
- Below 40: Poor visibility, major detection gaps

## Continuous Gap Monitoring

| Activity | Frequency | Owner |
|----------|-----------|-------|
| CMDB-to-EDR reconciliation | Weekly | Security operations |
| Log source health check | Daily (automated) | SIEM team |
| Sysmon/audit configuration audit | Monthly | Endpoint team |
| ATT&CK coverage mapping | Quarterly | Detection engineering |
| Telemetry coverage scorecard | Quarterly | Security leadership |
| New asset onboarding verification | Per deployment | Security operations |
| Agent version compliance | Weekly | Endpoint team |

## References

- MITRE ATT&CK Data Sources: https://attack.mitre.org/datasources/
- Sysmon Configuration Guide: https://github.com/SwiftOnSecurity/sysmon-config
- SANS Endpoint Visibility Guide
- CIS Controls v8: Control 8 (Audit Log Management)
- Roberto Rodriguez's HELK Project (detection telemetry)
- Palantir Windows Event Forwarding Guidance
